text:
There once existed a quaint town nestled between rolling hills. Every morning, the sun would paint the sky with pastels as it streaked across the dew-speckled grasses. In the town center stood an ancient oak tree where old tales were often whispered.

One serene morning, a little owl named Orion would sit on its broad branch, nibbling on a crumb. Orion had wings that shimmered like moonlight even in broad daylight. It was known far and wide for its nightly song, a soothing hum that would lull creatures to sleep.

However, on this particular day, Orion had been unusually preoccupied. Instead of its usual evening serenade, it hooted softly to itself. Curiosity piqued, Orion flew higher into the crowns of the trees until it spotted something unusual—a small flame flickering amidst the emerald shadows.

Approaching cautiously, Orion discovered a cozy little flame that hummed softly. By some magical trick—the flame had never needed fuel—its
